steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents a logical gap: all native fruit-eating birds are extinct, yet introduced birds are now spreading seeds for native plants that depended on native fruit-eating birds. The blank must complete the logical conclusion of this situation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — hwameis and other non-native birds play an important role that used to be filled by birds that were native to Oahu.: Directly follows from the evidence. Native birds are gone; introduced birds are now performing that seed-dispersal role. This is the logical conclusion the passage leads to. ✓
  • Choice B — non-native birds, such as the hwamei, will also be at risk of extinction if they lose access to kolea lau nui trees.: The passage says nothing about threats to non-native birds or any dependency they have on specific trees. Unsupported speculation. ✗
  • Choice C — populations of hwameis and other non-native birds will probably soon experience rapid growth.: No data in the passage supports population growth predictions for introduced birds. ✗
  • Choice D — non-native birds, such as the hwamei, now spread more seeds from the kolea lau nui tree than birds native to Oahu did in the past.: A quantity comparison (more seeds than before) goes beyond anything the passage states. ✗
That is the correct answer.
